selection_principles: Prefer mainstream, widely used, familiar brands or services from the provided options.
Prefer practical mid-range choices over niche, luxury, specialist, or extreme options.
For multi-select brand/tool questions, select a small plausible set rather than many options.
Do not select a brand only because it is famous if the option text does not fit the question.
If options include both very common and very niche brands, prefer the common ones.
If unsure, choose the safest ordinary-consumer answer.
experience_answer_principles: Avoid extreme praise or criticism unless the question clearly supports it.
Prefer answers about usefulness, clarity, time saved, reliability, setup effort, and ease of use.
Do not claim detailed first-hand experience with a specific product unless the question only asks for a general opinion or representative preference.

text_answer_style.length: short to medium unless the question asks for detail

selection_principles: Answer from practical professional judgement rather than claiming personal ownership or direct use.
Prefer tools, brands, or options that are common in professional software and digital product work.
Evaluate based on usefulness, reliability, clarity, integration, adoption effort, and productivity impact.
Avoid claiming direct hands-on experience unless explicitly listed in true_profile_facts.

allowed_research_contexts: professional research for technology and product evaluation
work-related research in software, automation, and AI tools
allowed_answer_style: Use broad role/category labels when the question allows it.
Prefer independent/self-directed/professional technology context over named institution.
Do not claim academic, medical, government, or named-institution affiliation unless explicitly listed.
requires_human_review_when: The question requires a named employer, university, lab, or institution.
The question asks for official employment status or legally identifying professional information.
The available options only include disallowed claims.

notes: For ordinary low-risk consumer questions, answer using representative_persona.
For professional or software/product questions, answer using professional_persona.
Use current question options as the main candidate space; do not rely on fixed brand lists.
Only require human review for strict private information, invalid option IDs, low confidence, or contradictions with session memory.
When asked about brands, tools, or services, choose plausible mainstream options from the provided choices and avoid selecting too many.
